field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: LCCC using RI detector as a concentration detector allowed separation and quantification of PIB species according to their chemical heterogeneity

conditions.physical_address: Polymer Science Program, Department of Chemistry, University of Massachusetts Lowell, One University Avenue, Lowell, Massachusetts 01854, USA; Division of Advanced Materials Science and Department of Chemistry, Pohang University of Science and Technology (POSTECH), Pohang, Pohang 790-784, South Korea
conditions.notes: RI detector used for quantitative analysis. ELS detector also used for LCCC but noted as non-linear.

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: According to Fig. 6 in THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w) PIB samples with identical chemical functionality eluted at the same position, independently of their MW.
field_evidence.critical_component: PIB samples with identical chemical functionality eluted at the same position, independently of their MW
field_evidence.column_name: One reversed phase C18 chromatography column (YMC HPLC COLUMN)
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: HPLC grade THF and methanol were used to prepare the mobile phase
field_evidence.mobile_phase_ratio: The critical solvent composition for PIB was established as THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w)
field_evidence.temperature_celsius: All measurements were carried out at a constant column temperature of 35[◦] C
field_evidence.flow_rate: at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min

conditions.notes: ELS detector used for LCCC analysis of PIB-diallyl. Note: ELS signal not strictly proportional to concentration.
